channel.title: Communications
channel.description: A RSS feed containing the latest articles published in Communications

item.title: Titelseiten
item.description: Article by Fang Chen, Jixian Pang DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/commun-2025-frontmatter1

item.title: Editorial 2025: A jubilee year for Communications

item.title: “It’s not us, it’s the government”: Perceptions of a national minority of their representations in the mainstream media during a global pandemic – the case of Israeli Arabs and COVID-19
item.description: Article by Fang Chen, Jixian Pang DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/commun-2022-0081

item.title: Mapping environment-focused social media, audiovisual media and art, in Sweden: How a diversity of voices and issues is combined with ideological homogeneity
item.description: Article by Fang Chen, Jixian Pang DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/commun-2022-0083

item.title: A normative perspective on information avoidance behaviors: Separating various types of avoidance-related norms
item.description: Article by Fang Chen, Jixian Pang DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/commun-2022-0085

item.title: From “screen time” to screen times: Measuring the temporality of media use in the messy reality of family life
item.description: Article by Fang Chen, Jixian Pang DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/commun-2022-0097

item.title: Bringing the campaign closer to the voters: Facebook in partisan-managed campaigning in France
item.description: Article by Fang Chen, Jixian Pang DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/commun-2022-0106

item.title: Sociotechnical infrastructuring for digital participation in rural development: A survey of public administrators in Germany
item.description: Article by Fang Chen, Jixian Pang DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/commun-2022-0107

item.title: The discursive construction of a news event: Access and legitimation in the media framing of an escalated anti-asylum protest in Belgium
item.description: Article by Fang Chen, Jixian Pang DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/commun-2022-0109

item.title: The contextual interplay between advertising and online disinformation: How brands suffer from and amplify deceptive content
item.description: Article by Fang Chen, Jixian Pang DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/commun-2022-0110
